scenarios.title: T1: College Weekend Group Booking
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11. Midterms just ended and I'm completely burnt out. My friends Taylor and Avery and I have been trying to hang out for weeks, but between classes and part-time jobs, nothing has worked out. This weekend is the first opening for all three of us — if not this weekend, it will be a while before our schedules align again. I told them I'd handle the booking.

We talked in our group chat last night and agreed on a comedy to blow off some steam. A coworker recently told me "Shared Shift" was hilarious, so I'm planning to look into that first. We're flexible, though, as long as it's a comedy movie. We'd prefer to avoid romantic comedies if possible, but we're open to it if the other options don't work out. I generally prefer movies with high ratings, so I'll lean toward whichever is best reviewed.

We live in a small college town in Virginia and none of us have cars, so the theater needs to be within 10 miles. Anything outside the town would not work. Sunday mornings are out because Avery goes to church. On Saturdays, I tend to sleep in, so morning showtimes are not ideal. Taylor is strict about his sleep schedule — he needs the movie to be one that ends before 10 PM, since he goes to bed at 10:30 every night.

The three of us must sit together in adjacent seats. Avery has low vision and prefers to sit in the second row — the first row is too close and causes neck pain, but anything beyond the third row is too far for her to see the screen comfortably.

scenarios.title: S1-T1: Parents' Anniversary Premium Seats
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. My parents' wedding anniversary is tomorrow, Thursday, March 12, and I want to surprise them with a movie date. I've been planning this for a while — I want the tickets to be for their actual anniversary tomorrow, not a make-up night later in the week. They both retired recently and have been saying they want to get out of the house more, so this feels like the perfect gift.

They take a long walk after lunch every day, so the show should start after 2 PM. They also like to be home in time to cook dinner together, so it needs to end before 6 PM. My mom has chronic back pain, so they need premium reclining seats with extra legroom — regular seats won't work for her. The two seats must be together, of course. I want the closest theater to make the trip easy for them. If multiple movies fit all of these constraints, I prefer the highest-rated one.

scenarios.title: S1-T2: Saturday Night Action With Spouse
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. My spouse and I have both had a long week, and we've been looking forward to a date night this Saturday, March 14. The booking site may show dates from Thursday, March 12 through Monday, March 16, but we specifically want this Saturday — it's the only night we're both free. It must be an action movie; we've both been craving something with great stunts and chase scenes. If there's more than one action movie available, I prefer the highest-rated one, but the timing and seating constraints matter more.

I don't get off work until 5 on Saturday, and the drive takes about 30 minutes, so the earliest I can arrive at the theater is 5:30 PM. My spouse gets up at 6 AM every day, so the movie must end before 10:30 PM or we'll both be exhausted the next morning. I need two adjacent seats, and I want to avoid the first three rows because sitting too close always gives me neck pain.

scenarios.title: S1-T3: Solo Weekend 3D Booking
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. A friend won't stop raving about how funny Cosmic Laughs is, and the trailer looked incredible, so I've decided to go watch Cosmic Laughs by myself this weekend. Either Saturday, March 14 or Sunday, March 15 works for me. The booking site may also show Monday, March 16 and Tuesday, March 17, but those dates do not work for me since I have to be at the office. I have dinner plans both evenings, so the showing needs to be one that finishes before 7 PM.

I'd like the closest theater if possible, but anywhere within 10 miles is fine. Anything outside the town would not work — I don't want to spend forever driving. The screening must be in 3D; I've heard the visual effects are stunning and I don't want to miss that. I also do not want a side seat — the 3D effect is terrible from an angle, so the seat needs to be reasonably centered on the screen.

scenarios.title: S2-T1: Child's Playdate Kid-Friendly Booking
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. My 8-year-old has a playdate this Sunday, March 15, with a friend from school, and I promised the kids I'd take them to a movie right afterward. The booking site may show Saturday, March 14 and Monday, March 16 through Tuesday, March 17 as well, but Sunday is the only day that works since that's when the playdate is. I need a kid-friendly movie appropriate for ages 7 to 9, since both children are in that range. If there's more than one option, I'd like something under 2 hours long — kids that age get fidgety during longer movies.

I want the closest theater to keep the drive short. The playdate wraps up around noon and we'll grab lunch first, so the show should start after 1 PM. I also need to have them home by dinner, so it should end before 5 PM. The two kids will sit next to each other — I need two adjacent seats for them, and the seats must not be in the first two rows because sitting that close hurts their necks.

scenarios.title: S2-T2: Friday Night Thriller With Sibling
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. My older sibling is visiting from out of state this week, and we want to watch a thriller movie together on Friday night, March 13 — it's the last evening before they fly home Saturday morning. The booking site may show dates from Thursday, March 12 through Monday, March 16, but Friday is the only night that works. If there's more than one thriller playing, I prefer the highest-rated one, but the timing and seating constraints come first.

I want the closest theater so we don't waste time driving. I'm at work until 5:30 and it takes about 30 minutes to get there, so the earliest I can arrive at the theater is 6:00 PM. We both need to be up early the next day, so the movie must be one that ends before 10 PM. I need two adjacent seats for us. My sibling has low vision, so we do not want to sit in the last two rows — they need to be close enough to read the screen clearly.

scenarios.title: S2-T3: Next Weekend Ocean Depths IMAX
scenarios.story:
Today is Wednesday, March 11, 2026. Last month I saw the trailer for a nature film and was completely blown away by the underwater cinematography. Ever since, I've been wanting to watch a documentary called Ocean Depths on the big screen. I'm planning to go alone next weekend, either Saturday, March 21 or Sunday, March 22. The booking site may also show Monday, March 23 and Tuesday, March 24, but those dates do not work for me. I have evening commitments both weekend days, so I'm unavailable after 8 PM on Saturday and Sunday.

I'd like the closest theater, but any theater within 12 miles is acceptable. A movie like this deserves the biggest screen possible, so the screening must be in IMAX. I want a seat in the center section for the best viewing angle, and it must not be in the first two rows — sitting that close to an IMAX screen would be overwhelming.
